name: 20 · Loki Query
description: Demo of the Loki/LogQL executor backend. Start collects {app, since}; a single Loki `query_range` step binds the workspace `loki` resource `demo_loki` and runs the LogQL query `{job="varlogs", app="{{ start.app }}"}` over the lookback window `{{ start.since }}` (e.g. `1h`) with `limit: 1000`, `direction: backward`. The `{{ start.app }}` value is spliced into the LogQL double-quoted matcher string through a LogQL-escaping render (the analog of binding Postgres params through $1) so an upstream value carrying a quote or backslash cannot break out of the matcher. The step resolves the bound resource via ResourceChannel::ConfigOverlay — `base_url` (required), optional bearer `token`, optional `X-Scope-OrgID` `org_id`. Output is the fixed Loki envelope: `entries` (array of {ts, line, labels} for `streams` results), `entry_count`, `series` (raw matrix/vector for metric queries), `result_type` ("streams" | "matrix" | "vector"), and `stats`. End maps `entries`, `entry_count`, and `result_type` onto the instance result. The `demo_loki` resource (`demos/resources/demo_loki.json`) points at a local, unauthenticated Loki at http://localhost:3100 (no token/org_id). IMPORTANT: before running this demo live you must push test log lines into Loki under the matched labels — e.g. POST to `http://localhost:3100/loki/api/v1/push` a stream `{job="varlogs", app="<app>"}` with one or more `[ts_ns, line]` values — otherwise the query returns zero entries and the `entry_count gte 1` assertion fails. Requires a `loki` resource named `demo_loki` in the workspace at /resources (auto-seeded from demos/resources/demo_loki.json).
